content.problemStatement:
Create a program that simulates a to-do list manager using a queue. 

The program should allow users to perform the following operations:


Add a task to the to-do list.
If the task is already present in the list - then ignore the task
Display the current to-do list once all tasks have been added
The code given here has implemented queues using arrays. 

Update the code to solve the problem.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the number of tasks to be added. The next N lines each contain a single integer task (1 ≤ task ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the distinct tasks in the order of their first appearance, each on its own line. Do not print extra blank lines between test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first occurrence of each distinct task is kept; later repetitions are ignored.
All inputs are the same, so only the first one is printed.
All tasks are distinct, so the output mirrors the input.

solution.approach: We maintain a boolean array (size 1001) or a hash set to remember which tasks have already been seen. While reading the N tasks we check the structure; if the task is new we store it in a list preserving the order. After processing a test case we output the stored list. This works because we only need to remember presence, not counts, and the order of first occurrence is naturally kept by appending when a new task appears. The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1000) extra space for the presence array (or O(K) for a set where K is the number of distinct tasks).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1000) auxiliary space (or O(K) for the set), plus O(K) for the output list.
